fear: That the falsified fire tables will be traced back to his own staff, implicating him in negligence or worse — destroying the reputation he has built his entire identity around.
name: Colonel Robert R. McCormick
voice: Clipped, declarative, newspaper-headline syntax. No subordinate clauses. Every sentence an order or a verdict.
secrets: He privately suspects Lieutenant Farrow has been reporting to a French intelligence contact, but has no proof and fears the diplomatic consequences of raising the accusation
The original fire tables he signed contained a minor calculation error that Farrow corrected — McCormick does not know whether the corrected version or the original is now in the guns
knowledge: He signed the original fire tables at 23:00 on May 27th and locked them in the map-table case
He knows Lieutenant Farrow was the last officer with access to the case before the 03:00 stand-to
He is aware that a French liaison officer, Captain Renard, requested a copy of the fire tables 'for coordination purposes' two days ago — a request McCormick denied
publicFace: A tall, imperious Chicago newspaper heir turned artillery officer — precise, demanding, and visibly contemptuous of anyone who cannot keep up with his technical standards. He runs his dugout with the rigid precision of both a professional officer and a newspaper executive accustomed to controlling information: every fact must be sourced, every number must check out.
represents: Artillery officers of the 5th Field Artillery, 1st Division, who coordinated fire support at Cantigny on May 28, 1918
trustLogic: Opens up when presented with documented, verifiable evidence — numbers, times, physical objects. Shuts down completely when approached with rumor or supposition. Respects anyone who has done their homework.
privateGoal: To prove — to Pershing, to the French, and to the Tribune's readership — that he personally helped win America's first battle. The after-action report he is already composing in his head will make his name.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Demands the questioner state their evidence in writing before he will respond
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never admit that he may have signed a document he did not fully verify
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Invokes his rank and threatens court-martial for insubordination while visibly sweating through his collar
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Becomes rigidly formal — name, rank, and a demand for a JAG officer
